unit.name: Transformer blocks and why transformers scale across domains
unit.description: Compares RNNs, convolutions, and attention, then assembles the transformer block and applies it to language models and vision transformers.
unit.summary: The lecture compares three sequence-processing primitives: RNNs are inherently sequential and hard to parallelize, convolutions are parallel but local, and self-attention gives global interaction in one layer at the cost of quadratic compute and memory in sequence length [ts=3510s][ts=3566s][ts=3606s][ts=3626s]. This leads directly to the transformer, which places self-attention at the center of a block with residual connections, layer normalization, and an MLP/FFN that processes each vector independently; stacking these blocks gives the full architecture [ts=3760s][ts=3776s][ts=3814s][ts=3845s]. The instructor then emphasizes that this same architecture scales from the original 2017 model to today’s massive systems and can be used for language modeling as well as vision by patchifying images, projecting each patch to a vector, running a transformer over patches, and pooling outputs for classification [ts=3854s][ts=3876s][ts=3885s][ts=3900s]. The segment closes by summarizing attention as a scalable primitive on sets of vectors and transformers as the backbone of modern large AI models [ts=3934s][ts=3950s].

key_points.text: RNNs are sequential, convolutions are local but parallel, and self-attention is global and parallelizable.
Self-attention has O(n^2) compute and memory in sequence length n.
A transformer uses self-attention as its main interaction mechanism over a set of vectors.
Residual connections and layer normalization wrap self-attention to stabilize training.
Each transformer block also includes an MLP/FFN that processes each vector independently.
A transformer is a stack of transformer blocks.
Vision Transformers split an image into patches, project each patch to a vector, run a transformer, then pool outputs for classification.
The lecture concludes that transformers are the backbone of large modern AI models.

kp_catalog.name: Transformer block architecture
kp_catalog.description: The same core block yields a decoder-only model with masking, an encoder-only model without masking, and an encoder-decoder model with cross-attention. A transformer block combines self-attention, residual connections, normalization, and an MLP/FFN, and a full transformer is a stack of these blocks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because transformer block architecture synthesizes the same core block yields a decoder-only model with masking, an encoder-only model without masking, and an encoder-decoder model with cross-attention into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe though importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Transformers as a cross-domain backbone
kp_catalog.description: The same transformer architecture supports next-token language models and vision transformers over image patches, and the lecture frames it as the dominant backbone for large AI models.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because transformers as a cross-domain backbone clarifies the same transformer architecture supports next-token language models and vision transformers over image patches, and the lecture frames it as the dominant backbone for large AI models, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Self-attention
kp_catalog.description: Self-attention applies attention within one set of vectors by projecting each input into a query, key, and value so every element can mix information from the whole set. Each token can gather information from every token in the sequence in a single parallel operation, rather than relying on stepwise recurrence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because self-attention formalizes how self-attention applies attention within one set of vectors by projecting each input into a query, key, and value so every element can mix information from the whole set, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
